w:r.w:t: Supplier & Exporter from India – Lightweight Fillers for Industrial Applications
Bulk supply for oilfield, construction, paints & plastics | Export-ready shipments
are lightweight, hollow, inert microspheres derived from coal fly ash, widely used as a filler material to reduce weight, improve strength, and enhance insulation properties.
form during the high-temperature combustion of pulverised coal at temperatures between 1,200°C and 1,700°C. The sequence of formation involves:
Coal particles melt and form molten droplets in the combustion zone.
, and steam) expand inside the molten droplets.
Surface tension retains the gas bubble, forming a hollow sphere.
Rapid cooling as the particles exit the furnace solidifies the shell, preserving the hollow structure.
Only a small fraction of fly ash — typically 0.1% to 2% by weight — consists of
Up to 150 MPa (varies by shell thickness)
in composition. The main chemical components are:
The enclosed gas is typically a mixture of nitrogen and carbon dioxide. The glassy amorphous shell gives
their chemical stability and resistance to most acids and alkalis.

are incorporated into lightweight concrete, plaster, and grouting compounds. They reduce the overall density of the material, improve workability, and enhance thermal and acoustic insulation without significantly compromising structural performance.

as a lightweight filler in oil well cement slurries. By reducing slurry density, they help control hydrostatic pressure during cementing operations in deep wells, reducing the risk of formation fracturing.
Their low density and mechanical strength make

ideal fillers in syntactic foams used for buoyancy modules, automotive body panels, underbody coatings, and ablative thermal protection systems. They contribute to weight reduction without sacrificing structural integrity.
When added to paints, primers, and coatings,
improve texture, reduce weight, lower material costs, and provide thermal insulating properties. They are widely used in exterior architectural coatings and marine applications.
As a functional filler in polymer matrices,
enhance stiffness, reduce shrinkage, and improve surface finish. They
in thermoplastic and thermoset composites for industrial, consumer, and sporting goods applications.
, kiln furniture, and insulation boards owing to their high melting point and low thermal conductivity. They are suitable for furnace linings and high-temperature industrial equipment.
India is one of the world's largest
exporters, supplying nearly a quarter of global demand. An extensive network of coal-fired thermal power plants ensures abundant, consistent supply of high-quality material.
are trusted by global buyers for their superior alumina-silica composition and consistent particle sizing

— qualities that frequently outperform competing origins. Strategically located, India offers shorter transit times to the Middle East, Southeast Asia, and beyond, making it the most cost-effective and reliable sourcing destination in the world.
We source directly from thermal power plants across India's major coal belts — no
intermediaries, no inflated costs, full traceability on every batch. Our direct TPP sourcing model ensures consistently priced, lab-tested

that meet your exact specifications. Backed by pre-established freight partnerships and streamlined export documentation, we move orders from plant to port faster than most. We supply buyers across the Middle East, USA, Europe, and Southeast Asia — and our clients keep coming back because we deliver on time, every time.
